prompt: Add a [project.optional-dependencies] section to click's pyproject.toml with a 'dev' group containing pytest, mypy, and pre-commit. Also update README.md to document the new dev install command (pip install -e .[dev]).

turns.content:
<task>Add a `[project.optional-dependencies]` section to click's `pyproject.toml` with a `dev` group containing `pytest`, `mypy`, and `pre-commit`. The existing `[dependency-groups]` section uses the newer PEP 735 format, but many users and CI systems still rely on the classic `pip install -e .[dev]` workflow, so we need the `[project.optional-dependencies]` section as well.

Also update `README.md` to add a "Development" section before the "Donate" section that documents how to install with dev dependencies using `pip install -e .[dev]`.</task>

EDIT TOOLS — Modifying existing files

TWO APPROACHES:

1. SYMBOLIC (preferred for methods/functions):
   edit_symbol(symbol, new_code)  — Replace a method/function by name
   add_symbol(code, file_path)    — Add a method to a file or class
   remove_symbol(symbol)          — Remove a method by name

2. LINE-BASED (for anything else):
   replace_lines(file_path, content, start_line, end_line)
   — Deterministic: specify exact line range, no text matching needed
   — Use read_file first to see line numbers

3. INSERT (add new content without removing):
   add_content_after_line(file_path, line_number, content)
   add_content_before_line(file_path, line_number, content)

4. REFACTORING (project-wide operations):
   rename_symbol(symbol, new_name)  — Rename everywhere: definition + all references + imports
   move_symbol(symbol, target_file) — Move to another file/class, update imports

WORKFLOW:
  1. read_file("src/foo.py")           → see the code with line numbers
  2. edit_symbol("Foo.bar", new_code)  → replace method by name
  OR
  2. replace_lines("src/foo.py", new_code, 10, 25)  → replace lines 10-25

turns.content: [Thought recorded]

1
2 3 # Click 4 5 Click is a Python package for creating beautiful command line interfaces 6 in a composable way with as little code as necessary. It's the "Command 7 Line Interface Creation Kit". It's highly configurable but comes with 8 sensible defaults out of the box. 9 10 It aims to make the process of writing command line tools quick and fun 11 while also preventing any frustration caused by the inability to 12 implement an intended CLI API. 13 14 Click in three points: 15 16 - Arbitrary nesting of commands 17 - Automatic help page generation 18 - Supports lazy loading of subcommands at runtime 19 20 21 ## A Simple Example 22 23 ```python 24 import click 25 26 @click.command() 27 @click.option("--count", default=1, help="Number of greetings.") 28 @click.option("--name", prompt="Your name", help="The person to greet.") 29 def hello(count, name): 30 """Simple program that greets NAME for a total of COUNT times.""" 31 for _ in range(count): 32 click.echo(f"Hello, {name}!") 33 34 if __name__ == '__main__': 35 hello() 36 ``` 37 38 ``` 39 $ python hello.py --count=3 40 Your name: Click 41 Hello, Click! 42 Hello, Click! 43 Hello, Click! 44 ``` 45 46 47 ## Donate 48 49 The Pallets organization develops and supports Click and other popular 50 packages. In order to grow the community of contributors and users, and 51 allow the maintainers to devote more time to the projects, [please 52 donatetoday][]. 53 54 [please donate today]: https://palletsprojects.com/donate 55 56 ## Contributing 57 58 See our [detailed contributing documentation][contrib] for many ways to 59 contribute, including reporting issues, requesting features, asking or answering 60 questions, and making PRs. 61 62 [contrib]: https://palletsprojects.com/contributing/

arguments.final_answer:
Added `[project.optional-dependencies]` section to `pyproject.toml` with a `dev` group containing pytest, mypy, and pre-commit. The section is placed between `[project]` metadata and `[project.urls]` following standard pyproject.toml ordering.

Also added a "Development" section to `README.md` (before the Donate section) documenting the `pip install -e .[dev]` command for installing with dev dependencies.

Verified the configuration works: `pip install -e .[dev]` successfully installs all three packages.
